{"repository_id":700648272,"push_id":20722734561,"size":9,"distinct_size":9,"ref":"refs/heads/julia1-compat","head":"2b9d72f81f55b23c01486a3b152a5f3464d51657","before":"1b38e5376de0194e7df012bc0d0f99b4df7f5029","commits":[{"sha":"4839469214dcc7e67777f7079ace228cdc47ca52","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Consistent Face hashes

Since we implement a custom equality function, it seems nice to have it
so that Face objects that are equal hash equally too.

I'm not aware of anybody using this, but it seems nice to have.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/4839469214dcc7e67777f7079ace228cdc47ca52"},{"sha":"556470ed8572c4014e10cd5a51de7a120a3c3401","author":{"email":"3248bdb596efdfcbcec787fd1cd80737324ac4ef@hotmail.fr","name":"serenity4"},"message":"More meticulously check indices in eachregion

Instead of just assuming that pos-1 is a valid string index, along with
all other regions, use lastind/thisind to ensure they are.

Co-authored-by: TEC <git@tecosaur.net>","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/556470ed8572c4014e10cd5a51de7a120a3c3401"},{"sha":"d63fbd75f1dd7b8b98b0612478435a1b442912de","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Improve type stability within styled"" macro

This way, when interpolating, the type of the expression can be inferred
as an AnnotatedString, rather than a Union{String, AnnotatedString}.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/d63fbd75f1dd7b8b98b0612478435a1b442912de"},{"sha":"bc03ce22a6a31556175da1c3c25e42d84900f942","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Remove tag wrapping from HTML show method

It's an undocumented extension to Base.show, and can be done by the
caller.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/bc03ce22a6a31556175da1c3c25e42d84900f942"},{"sha":"6689845f35af7c21671f26e9000c8d5089a73f8d","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Fix syntax of generated CSS

Quoting and consistent semicolon separation weren't quite right, but
should be now.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/6689845f35af7c21671f26e9000c8d5089a73f8d"},{"sha":"7154ea81a0100b894892700f11cf5efe36f04cab","author":{"email":"885938df3492f645023ddd000b8cc4c1969f7cad@juliacomputing.com","name":"KristofferC"},"message":"Mark API as Experimental

Given the ongoing discussion about how exactly bits of this library
should work (namely the Faces system), it's worth declaring that the API
is experimental, so it can stabilize while also being used across the
stdlib.

The mention of AnnotatedStrings is slightly premature, but we're about
to bring that in as part of what StyledStrings claims API responsibility
for. This is in many ways the main motivation for this notice.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/7154ea81a0100b894892700f11cf5efe36f04cab"},{"sha":"8eaf6f941120a7d291d1311d8484ec3f5c6b5ba3","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Fix markdown syntax in "Mark API as experimental"

Four spaces are needed in admonitions, not two.

Amends 8332e457e11fc3feb81ff9d8a1eaf6a500e57124.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/8eaf6f941120a7d291d1311d8484ec3f5c6b5ba3"},{"sha":"9e6095bfeb446c1c61951bd2c730b67952ac3c52","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Call load_customisations! automatically, lazily

I've been wary about doing lazy application of load_customisations!, but
with the @noinline addition the calls are really cheap: only 1.5ns
according to Chairmarks on my machine. Thus, the cost of calling it
after initialisation will be negligeable for any non-trivial function.

Having rolled this around further, other than printing I think only
withfaces calls actually require loading of user styles to be valid.

With these two realisations, calling load_customisations! lazily as
appropriate seems like a bit of a no-brainer.

We'll probably want to add this to public API eventually for
non-text/html output implementations, but we can wait to see how this is
bourne out first.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/9e6095bfeb446c1c61951bd2c730b67952ac3c52"},{"sha":"2b9d72f81f55b23c01486a3b152a5f3464d51657","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Adopt Base's annotated types/functions as API

This opens the door to shuffling parts currently implemented in Base
into this stdlib without breaking any public APIs.

It would be nice to actually show the relevant Annotated* docstrings,
but unfortunately due to
https://github.com/JuliaDocs/Documenter.jl/issues/1781 it's a bit
difficult to actually do so. We should re-visit this later.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/2b9d72f81f55b23c01486a3b152a5f3464d51657"}]}

These are different things. ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1692ae90-5917-4c98-b381-4cd8c2e8a839) To me this seems like predictable, consistent behaviour. If I'm missing something do elaborate 🙂. I suspect you may have intended the example not to show "surprising behaviour" but to show a perceived difference in behaviour when printing compared to `merge` + `getface`? In that case, I see what you mean and see how one could expect `merge(getface(:a), getface(:b), getface(:c))` to be equivalent to `getface([:a, :b, :c])` ... but this is not the case. This removes a ton of implicit converts from this code, which are easily
invalidated.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/729f56cb7c7064687a19ed02990a532606d83e4c"},{"sha":"3f2a7c33ad036bdb95e5fba5fff41881d59b02ba","author":{"email":"308377d01bcb9d4e526ee30856be1f850fe7e000@tapscott.me","name":"Cody Tapscott"},"message":"Resolve `Face` eagerly when constructing StyledString

This is step 1 to eliminating our type-piracy problems

StyledStrings needs to put some type that it owns into its AnnotatedStrings
so that we have a right to hook into the display logic (and the display
logic can know which copy of StyledStrings to delegate to).

It is also a semantic change to how constructing StyledStrings
behaves, but overall I think it's a lot more intuitive to have a
StyledString actually compute its style information at construction

Resolves #87","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/3f2a7c33ad036bdb95e5fba5fff41881d59b02ba"}]}

I'm yet to go through all of your recent flurry of PRs, but from this and a few others, I've developed two linked concerns that I think are worth discussing.

Both concerns stem from the more eager use of `getface`. The obvious (and seemingly intended) consequence of this is that details are resolved when the `StyledString` is constructed. There are two implications of this I am least wary of.

### Baking in different values, unexpectedly

Firstly, if a string is constructed at compile time/before customisations are loaded, a later use of the same face can look different. Here's an example to illustrate:

```julia
# in some package Foo

@define_faces!(:my_foo => Face(foreground=:red, underline=:yellow)) # Assuming future work

const reused_string_or_similar = styled"... {my_foo:stuff} ..."

function something_a(io::IO)
    ...
    println(io, reused_string_or_similar)
    ...
end

function something_b(io::IO)
    ...
    println(io, styled"... {my_foo:other stuff} ...")
    ...
end
```

In this scenario, should `my_foo` be customised then `something_a` differ from `something_b` in output style, in what I'd consider a surprising way.

### Inability to customise downstream

One of the features of the current design is to remap faces on the fly. There are particular use-cases I see this enabling. For instance, say `JuliaSyntaxHighlighting` is used to bake in static highlighting (an idea that's of some interest to the Documenter crowd). To do this, and produce output for all the various themes you currently could do something like:

```julia
const DOCUMENTER_THEME_X = Dict(:julia_number => Face(...), ...)
const DOCUMENTER_THEME_Y = Dict(:julia_number => Face(...), ...)

# ...

function html_highlight_julia(code::String, themes::Vector{Dict{Symbol, Face}})
    hl_code = highlight(code)
    map(themes) do theme
        withfaces(theme) do
            sprint(show, MIME("text/html"), hl_code)
        end
    end
end
```

Here the code is highlighted once, and then styled with multiple different themes. From what I can tell, this pattern wouldn't be possible with this PR. In this particular example, you could accept the overhead to re-parse the code and apply the faces for each theme before display, but are also other cases where you might want to pass some content across functions/packages this would be a more major impediment.

### Closing thoughts

I see how this gets us closer to avoiding type piracy by avoiding a Base-owned type as the value, making it possible to call the annotation owner's specialised printing function.

However, I'm not quite onboard with the specific tradeoffs made here, and wonder if there's a better balance that could be struck. Considering this together with our discussions on effectively implementing custom face namespaces, I wonder if a `FaceRef` type or similar could be introduced to allow us to avoid the problems with `Symbol`, without the consequences I've outlined above.

Indeed, but that doesn't prevent the behaviour I describe.

> For me, it's just as surprising that this doesn't work:

I'm not quite sure what you're referring to here, if we presume `@define_faces!` acts like a better version of the `addface!` of today, `getface(:my_foo)` works.

> I think that use case can be handled just by a LazyStyledString equivalent of some kind? I think the lazy approach is OK, but it should be specifically opt-in and a FaceRef would be a useful type to implement it internally.

We could branch out into lazy and non-lazy versions, but if we can reasonably get away with just one that seems preferable to me.

> The `merge(...)` we have right now partially resolves inheritance as part of its implementation, but that's not the right behavior since it means you stop seeing future updates to inherited Faces and freeze their definition (but only sometimes). To make these fully lazy, you need to defer the resolution of inheritance as well, which adds a lot of additional complexity / inefficiency.

I don't quite see the respect in which inheritance is non-lazy currently, could you elaborate?

> The eager solution lets you perform the `merge` up-front once and optimize on that concrete style info - that is a good thing IMO.

I'm tempted to see how far we can get by having a single lazy form, but offering a `freeze`/`bake`/etc. function that fully resolves all faces.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/comments/2417235445/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

This problem is exacerbated when Julia is launched with no colour output.

Current progress bar in VSCode (examples 1/2):
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b99640e-6fe5-4360-9add-5582fb8ff0a6)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a3288323-997e-42b8-9154-9fd067bed7ba)

I looked at how PIP deals with no colour since this design was inspired off theirs, they replace the unfilled side with white space to ensure good contrast.

Example 3:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d25057a6-8922-4621-bd34-ff97879dccf2)

Another idea I had would be to keep using the heavy character variant on the left, but use the light variant on the right. That would add even more differentiation while still looking similar to the new style.

Examples 4/5:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/659c3875-a41b-4290-b219-c0af038bcee7)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e0e52f12-970a-4072-91ec-b63ed85e4024)

Personally, I think the no colour option should use whitespace on the right (example 3), while for a colour terminal, I don't know if I prefer the current status bar or my suggestion (1 or issue has two parts.

# Issue 1

The following NEWS entry is incorrect:
> If no path is given after (i.e. `--project=@script`) then (like `--project=@.`) the directory and its parents are searched for a Project.toml

In particular, it appears `--project=@script` has the same behavior as `--project=@.`, which doesn't seem intended (though maybe I'm reading the NEWS entry incorrectly).

## MWE

In `<current_directory>/script_test/test.jl`:
```julia
println("Current directory: ", pwd())
println("Active project: ", Base.active_project())
```

Result:
```bash
$ julia +1.11 --project=@script script_test/test.jl
Current directory: <current_directory>
Active project: <current_directory>/Project.toml # Expected: <current_directory>/script_test/Project.toml
```

See #55069 for more details.

# Issue 2

The previous part of the same NEWS entry is also incorrect:
> The `--project` argument now accepts `@script` to give a path to a directory with a Project.toml relative to the passed script file. `--project=@script/foo` for the `foo` subdirectory.

In particular, `--project=@script/script_test` activates `/script_test/Project.toml`. (Note this is an absolute path, not a relative path!)

## MWE

Same `test.jl` file as above.

Result:
```bash
$ julia +1.11 --project=@script/foo script_test/test.jl
Current directory: <current_directory>
Active project: /script_test/Project.toml # Expected: <current_directory>/script_test/foo/Project.toml
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55606/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55606/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2427315247","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55606#issuecomment-2427315247","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55606","id":2427315247,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6QreQv","user":{"login":"tecosaur","id":20903656,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwOTAzNjU2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20903656?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur","html_url":"https://github.com/tecosaur","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-21T17:27:49Z","updated_at":"2024-10-21T17:27:49Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Thanks for reporting! behavior"},{"id":909267847,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw5MDkyNjc4NDc=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/duplicate","name":"duplicate","color":"b5cca8","default":true,"description":"Indicates requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2024-08-27T19:32:45Z","updated_at":"2024-10-21T18:44:59Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This issue has two parts.

# Issue 1

The following NEWS entry is incorrect:
> If no path is given after (i.e. `--project=@script`) then (like `--project=@.`) the directory and its parents are searched for a Project.toml

In particular, it appears `--project=@script` has the same behavior as `--project=@.`, which doesn't seem intended (though maybe I'm reading the NEWS entry incorrectly).

## MWE

In `<current_directory>/script_test/test.jl`:
```julia
println("Current directory: ", pwd())
println("Active project: ", Base.active_project())
```

Result:
```bash
$ julia +1.11 --project=@script script_test/test.jl
Current directory: <current_directory>
Active project: <current_directory>/Project.toml # Expected: <current_directory>/script_test/Project.toml
```

See #55069 for more details.

# Issue 2

The previous part of the same NEWS entry is also incorrect:
> The `--project` argument now accepts `@script` to give a path to a directory with a Project.toml relative to the passed script file. `--project=@script/foo` for the `foo` subdirectory.

In particular, `--project=@script/script_test` activates `/script_test/Project.toml`. (Note this is an absolute path, not a relative path!)

## MWE

Same `test.jl` file as above.

Result:
```bash
$ julia +1.11 --project=@script/foo script_test/test.jl
Current directory: <current_directory>
Active project: /script_test/Project.toml # Expected: <current_directory>/script_test/foo/Project.toml
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55606/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55606/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"reopened"}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/87","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/87/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/87/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/87/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/87","id":2504212347,"node_id":"I_kwDOKcMLUM6VQz97","number":87,"title":"`withfaces` doesn't apply to constructed StyledStrings","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-09-04T03:15:33Z","updated_at":"2024-09-04T03:48:22Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I'd have expected styling to be expected at string construction, so that:
```julia
const alert_msg = withfaces(:banner => Face(foreground = :red)) do
    styled"{banner:text}"
end
println(alert_msg)
```

prints "text" in red. Instead, it prints without styling.

IMO this is a subtle but important difference between what I'd expect to be a StyledString vs. an AnnotatedString.

If you want to carry around _style_ information with your string (I almost always do), I have to shuttle around the Faces I need to the presentation layer somehow - which is especially unclear how to do if I wanted to do `AbstractString`-style operations on this in a way that preserves styling.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/87/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/87/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/comments/2327857133","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/87#issuecomment-2327857133","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/87","id":2327857133,"node_id":"IC_kwDOKcMLUM6KwEft","user":{"login":"tecosaur","id":20903656,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwOTAzNjU2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20903656?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur","html_url":"https://github.com/tecosaur","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-04T03:48:21Z","updated_at":"2024-09-04T03:48:21Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"Mmmm, from this I take it that some of the nuances with the system could be better explained in the docs. I can imagine how one might imagine `withfaces` to remap named faces seen within it. This reminds me of an extra utility that has come to my mind: a function like `freezefaces(::AnnotatedString)` which resolves all named faces into standalone `Face` objects.

Let's add this to the discussion we've lined up. Just so it's out of Slack, I'll also reproduce the list of benefits/annoyances of the current system I wrote and you suggested edits to.

Key points I like with the current system:

- Straightforward user-customisation of all named faces
- Composability across packages
- The ability to remap faces in certain contexts
- Being able to weakly depend on other package's faces
- Concise usage

Key points of annoyance with the current system:

- Global mutable state
- Requires `__init__`-y stuff
- Uses a TOML parser","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/comments/2327857133/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

is IMO quite unintuitive and confusing to read:
```julia
julia> s = Base.AnnotatedString("this is an example annotated string", [(1:18, :A => 111), (12:28, :B => 222), (18:35, :C => 333)])

julia> Base.annotations(s)[2][1]
12:28

julia> Base.annotations(s)[2][2][1]  # or Base.annotations(s)[2][2].first
:B

julia> Base.annotations(s)[2][2][2]  # or Base.annotations(s)[2][2].second
222
```
Would be much nicer and self-explanatory to use meaningful names instead of numbers:
```julia
julia> Base.annotations(s)[2].region
12:28

julia> Base.annotations(s)[2].label
:B

julia> Base.annotations(s)[2].value
222
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55249/reactions","total_count":6,"+1":6,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55249/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2335052129","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55249#issuecomment-2335052129","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55249","id":2335052129,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6LLhFh","user":{"login":"tecosaur","id":20903656,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwOTAzNjU2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20903656?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur","html_url":"https://github.com/tecosaur","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-07T05:38:20Z","updated_at":"2024-09-07T05:38:20Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I think more than anything else this illustrates the problem with:

some simple precompile statement (and backport to 1.11 would be great!), then alternative to https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/pull/4015, to fix (3x PythonCall startup regression and) 16x for its dependency https://github.com/JuliaPy/CondaPkg.jl/issues/145

Pkg.REPLMode.gen_help() is slow (makes CondaPkg 16x slower, and therefore PythonCall startup), because of md_str macro taking:

```
julia> @time Markdown.mdexpr(" ... ")
  1.846971 seconds (794.50 k allocations: 40.745 MiB, 5.85% gc time, 99.96% compilation time)
:($(Expr(:escape, :((Markdown.MD)(Base.getindex(Any, (Markdown.Paragraph)(Base.getindex(Any, "... "))), Dict{Symbol, Any}(:config => Markdown.Config(Function[Markdown.hashheader, Markdown.list, Markdown.fencedcode, Markdown.blockquote, Markdown.admonition], Function[Markdown.blocktex, Markdown.blockinterp, Markdown.indentcode, Markdown.footnote, Markdown.github_table, Markdown.horizontalrule, Markdown.setextheader, Markdown.paragraph], Dict{Char, Vector{Function}}('!' => [Markdown.image], '_' => [Markdown.underscore_bold, Markdown.underscore_italic], '<' => [Markdown.autolink], '`' => [Markdown.inline_code], '*' => [Markdown.asterisk_bold, Markdown.asterisk_italic], '[' => [Markdown.footnote_link, Markdown.link], '\\' => [Markdown.linebreak, Markdown.escapes], '$' => [Markdown.tex, Markdown.interp], '-' => [Markdown.en_dash]))))))))

The ineffective way to time this was:

julia> @time @time md" ... "
  0.000002 seconds (6 allocations: 176 bytes)
  0.013056 seconds (219 allocations: 9.352 KiB, 98.93% compilation time)

also before that, this slow:

julia> @time using Markdown
  0.021316 seconds (8.72 k allocations: 818.078 KiB)

vs. on 1.10:

julia> @time using Markdown
  0.000452 seconds (413 allocations: 44.297 KiB)
```

When doing it command-line like this I see a whole bunch of uncompiled stuff, as in Mose's message.

For fun, I also tried nightly, and it performs similarly to 1.11. On nightly I can do `--trace-compile-timing` though, which might shed some more light on where this latency spike is coming from:

```julia
~$ time julia +nightly --startup-file=no -e 'using Markdown; @time @eval Markdown.mdexpr(" ... ")' --trace-compile=/tmp/julia_precompile.jl --trace-compile-timing
  0.771441 seconds (1.30 M allocations: 68.541 MiB, 0.99% gc time, 99.29% compilation time: <1% of which was recompilation)
user=1.86s system=0.05s cpu=178% total=1.070

~$ cat /tmp/julia_precompile.jl

both 1.11rc and nightly:
```julia
julia> using InlineStrings

julia> a = Base.AnnotatedString("annotated", [(1:9, :label => 1)])

julia> b = InlineString("abc")

# correct:
julia> a * b
"annotatedabc"

# wrong:
julia> join([a,b])
"annotated\x03cba"

# correct:
julia> join([String(a), b])
"annotatedabc"
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55198/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55198/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

{"repository_id":700648272,"push_id":20134523727,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/tiny-macro-opt","head":"03211c9257c1cf23fc66303582a5e349fd8fa498","before":"534a0223c4fe5faeca85e44229d0ce90820b4684","commits":[{"sha":"03211c9257c1cf23fc66303582a5e349fd8fa498","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Small optimisation to single-interpolation styled

When a single styled"{$face:$content}" form is used, this optimisation
makes the macroexpansion equivalent to handwritten code for this case,
as the annotatedstring_optimize! call is superfluous when there is only
one part.

While we're at it, we may as well make annotatedstring_optimize! more
efficient for the zero and one annotation cases.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/03211c9257c1cf23fc66303582a5e349fd8fa498"}]}

red_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DilumAluthge/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DilumAluthge/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DilumAluthge/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DilumAluthge/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DilumAluthge/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DilumAluthge/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":1,"closed_issues":126,"state":"open","created_at":"2022-08-25T21:39:30Z","updated_at":"2024-09-06T21:45:55Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":20,"created_at":"2024-07-25T13:11:39Z","updated_at":"2024-09-08T18:28:07Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This is IMO quite unintuitive and confusing to read:
```julia
julia> s = Base.AnnotatedString("this is an example annotated string", [(1:18, :A => 111), (12:28, :B => 222), (18:35, :C => 333)])

julia> Base.annotations(s)[2][1]
12:28

julia> Base.annotations(s)[2][2][1]  # or Base.annotations(s)[2][2].first
:B

julia> Base.annotations(s)[2][2][2]  # or Base.annotations(s)[2][2].second
222
```
Would be much nicer and self-explanatory to use meaningful names instead of numbers:
```julia
julia> Base.annotations(s)[2].region
12:28

julia> Base.annotations(s)[2].label
:B

julia> Base.annotations(s)[2].value
222
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55249/reactions","total_count":6,"+1":6,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55249/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2336780155","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55249#issuecomment-2336780155","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55249","id":2336780155,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6LSG97","user":{"login":"tecosaur","id":20903656,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwOTAzNjU2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20903656?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur","html_url":"https://github.com/tecosaur","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-08T18:28:06Z","updated_at":"2024-09-08T18:28:06Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Update: I think I have this implemented now, along with the required changes to StyledStrings and JuliaSyntaxHighlighting. is primarily motivated by reducing the amount of work that trimming is unable to remove.

It will require applications (by which I mean the actual thing that uses `StyledStrings`, for example the REPL) to call `load_customisations!` itself. If there's a good spot to add that so it automatically gets done "at the right time, when appropriate to do so" that would be even better, but I'm not sure there's an obvious spot that can be done without the potential for headaches (e.g. what if it comes up within a `withfaces` call).

For now at least, perhaps this is a reasonable compromise.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/79/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/79/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/comments/2340081418","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/pull/79#issuecomment-2340081418","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/79","id":2340081418,"node_id":"IC_kwDOKcMLUM6Les8K","user":{"login":"tecosaur","id":20903656,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwOTAzNjU2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20903656?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur","html_url":"https://github.com/tecosaur","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-10T09:00:39Z","updated_at":"2024-09-10T09:00:39Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"Cody and I have have had a chat about the concerns he raised in the above comments, and I think it's fair to say that we both see the single global faces dict as non-ideal, but not a terrible compromise. He's proposed an idea of "[Palettes](https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/87#issuecomment-2328956848)" that we're both interested in exploring and could come along in the future.

This is separate to the near-term future of the changes that Kristoffer has expressed dissatisfaction with. From here, our options are to:

1. Make no further changes
2. Revert this PR
3. Use a compile-time preference (can we do this reasonably without Preferences.jl, given it's not in the stdlib?), to set whether `load_customisations!` is called on init or not
4. Come up with a better balance of concerns/behaviour

The relevant considerations I'm aware of currently are:
- Face customisations are done via parsing a TOML file
- This was done unconditionally as an `__init__` step
- For static compiling in particular, we want it to be possible to not do this
- `load_customisations!` achieves this, but runs the risk of non-REPL-run/static-compiled code behaving differently to how people expect

This makes me wonder if perhaps a good (4) would be something like this (it it possible?)

```julia
@static if !we_are_static_compiling()
    const __init__ = load_customisations!
end
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/comments/2340081418/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"repository_id":700648272,"push_id":20315796428,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/kc/experimental","head":"8332e457e11fc3feb81ff9d8a1eaf6a500e57124","before":"79e842ccb75cca0a4a44143c1a4141fe6fc28743","commits":[{"sha":"8332e457e11fc3feb81ff9d8a1eaf6a500e57124","author":{"email":"885938df3492f645023ddd000b8cc4c1969f7cad@juliacomputing.com","name":"KristofferC"},"message":"Mark API as Experimental

Given the ongoing discussion about how exactly bits of this library
should work (namely the Faces system), it's worth declaring that the API
is experimental, so it can stabilize while also being used across the
stdlib.

The mention of AnnotatedStrings is slightly premature, but we're about
to bring that in as part of what StyledStrings claims API responsibility

{"repository_id":700648272,"push_id":20315803696,"size":1,"distinct_size":0,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"8332e457e11fc3feb81ff9d8a1eaf6a500e57124","before":"cfcfe8ce9f48e672496001dcfcd447890a7dedd6","commits":[{"sha":"8332e457e11fc3feb81ff9d8a1eaf6a500e57124","author":{"email":"885938df3492f645023ddd000b8cc4c1969f7cad@juliacomputing.com","name":"KristofferC"},"message":"Mark API as Experimental

Given the ongoing discussion about how exactly bits of this library
should work (namely the Faces system), it's worth declaring that the API
is experimental, so it can stabilize while also being used across the
stdlib.

The mention of AnnotatedStrings is slightly premature, but we're about
to bring that in as part of what StyledStrings claims API responsibility
for. This is in many ways the main motivation for this notice.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/8332e457e11fc3feb81ff9d8a1eaf6a500e57124"}]}

{"repository_id":700648272,"push_id":20335555361,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/lazy-init","head":"da41b6a8ea4d4e355159e9c791111c445cc6805c","before":"82d56dfc311884763754870adadd00f4928ee28e","commits":[{"sha":"da41b6a8ea4d4e355159e9c791111c445cc6805c","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Call load_customisations! automatically, lazily

I've been wary about doing lazy application of load_customisations!, but
with the @noinline addition the calls are really cheap: only 1.5ns
according to Chairmarks on my machine. Thus, the cost of calling it
after initialisation will be negligeable for any non-trivial function.

Having rolled this around further, other than printing I think only
withfaces calls actually require loading of user styles to be valid.

With these two realisations, calling load_customisations! lazily as
appropriate seems like a bit of a no-brainer.

We'll probably want to add this to public API eventually for
non-text/html output implementations, but we can wait to see how this is
bourne out first.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/da41b6a8ea4d4e355159e9c791111c445cc6805c"}]}

{"repository_id":700648272,"push_id":20335733354,"size":1,"distinct_size":0,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"da41b6a8ea4d4e355159e9c791111c445cc6805c","before":"af972e0a772069b4996cdd206d4e36defebd2453","commits":[{"sha":"da41b6a8ea4d4e355159e9c791111c445cc6805c","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Call load_customisations! automatically, lazily

I've been wary about doing lazy application of load_customisations!, but
with the @noinline addition the calls are really cheap: only 1.5ns
according to Chairmarks on my machine. Thus, the cost of calling it
after initialisation will be negligeable for any non-trivial function.

Having rolled this around further, other than printing I think only
withfaces calls actually require loading of user styles to be valid.

With these two realisations, calling load_customisations! lazily as
appropriate seems like a bit of a no-brainer.

{"repository_id":700648272,"push_id":20335743077,"size":4,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/annot-api-change","head":"237d2c7976a2205694b2a82183f30c1c26c56b1f","before":"91a0c6daefc2e7df545f7a9265572332b8a30291","commits":[{"sha":"8332e457e11fc3feb81ff9d8a1eaf6a500e57124","author":{"email":"885938df3492f645023ddd000b8cc4c1969f7cad@juliacomputing.com","name":"KristofferC"},"message":"Mark API as Experimental

Given the ongoing discussion about how exactly bits of this library
should work (namely the Faces system), it's worth declaring that the API
is experimental, so it can stabilize while also being used across the
stdlib.

The mention of AnnotatedStrings is slightly premature, but we're about
to bring that in as part of what StyledStrings claims API responsibility
for. This is in many ways the main motivation for this notice.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/8332e457e11fc3feb81ff9d8a1eaf6a500e57124"},{"sha":"af972e0a772069b4996cdd206d4e36defebd2453","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Fix markdown syntax in "Mark API as experimental"

Four spaces are needed in admonitions, not two.

Amends 8332e457e11fc3feb81ff9d8a1eaf6a500e57124.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/af972e0a772069b4996cdd206d4e36defebd2453"},{"sha":"da41b6a8ea4d4e355159e9c791111c445cc6805c","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Call load_customisations! automatically, lazily

I've been wary about doing lazy application of load_customisations!, but
with the @noinline addition the calls are really cheap: only 1.5ns
according to Chairmarks on my machine. Thus, the cost of calling it
after initialisation will be negligeable for any non-trivial function.

Having rolled this around further, other than printing I think only
withfaces calls actually require loading of user styles to be valid.

With these two realisations, calling load_customisations! lazily as
appropriate seems like a bit of a no-brainer.

We'll probably want to add this to public API eventually for
non-text/html output implementations, but we can wait to see how this is
bourne out first.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/da41b6a8ea4d4e355159e9c791111c445cc6805c"},{"sha":"237d2c7976a2205694b2a82183f30c1c26c56b1f","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Adjust to change of annotations type in Base

By popular demand, the type of an annotation is changing from a
Tuple{UnitRange{Int}, Pair{Symbol, Any}} to a
NamedTuple{(:region, :label, :value), Tuple{UnitRange{Int}, Symbol,
Any}}.

This necessitates some adjustments in the StyledStrings codebase.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/237d2c7976a2205694b2a82183f30c1c26c56b1f"}]}

This requires the expected code churn to `strings/annotated.jl`, and some changes to the StyledStrings and JuliaSyntaxHighlighting libraries.

a few places across Base and the stdlib, we emit paths that we like people to be able to click on in their terminal and editor. Up to this point, we have relied on auto-filepath detection, but this does not allow for alternative link text, such as contracted paths.

Doing so (via OSC 8 terminal links for example) requires filepath URI encoding.

This functionality was previously part of a PR modifying stacktrace printing (#51816), but after that became held up for unrelated reasons and another PR appeared that would benefit from this utility (#55335), I've split out this functionality so it can be used before the stacktrace printing PR is patterns","user":{"login":"vtjnash","id":330950,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzMDk1MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/330950?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash","html_url":"https://github.com/vtjnash","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This adds 3 new types, to conveniently express 3 common concurrent code patterns:

 - `PerProcess`: an action that must be taken once per process
 - `PerThread`: an action that must be taken once per thread id
 - `PerTask`: an action that must be take once per task object

The PerProcess object should replace `__init__` or similar hand rolled implementations of this.
The PerThread object should replace code that used to use `nthreads()` to implement a much less correct version of this (though this is not recommended in most new code, some foreign libraries may need this to interact well with C).
The PerTask object is simply a thin wrapper over `task_local_storage()`.

For example, the jldoctest demonstrates how to lazily allocate a shared constant vector unique to the process:

```julia
julia> const global_state = Base.PerProcess{Vector{Int}}() do
           println("Making lazy global value...done.")
           return [rand()]
       end;

julia> a = global_state();
Making lazy global value...done.

julia> a === global_state()
true

julia> a === fetch(@async global_state())
true
```","created_at":"2024-09-17T14:17:40Z","updated_at":"2024-09-25T09:32:27Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"2406acc5e7b361973f7e8d6caa78ce3eebe93cc3","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[{"login":"gbaraldi","id":28694980,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk0OTgw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/28694980?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi","html_url":"https://github.com/gbaraldi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false}],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[{"id":9159719,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw5MTU5NzE5","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/domain:parallelism","name":"domain:parallelism","color":"f7e102","default":false,"description":"Parallel {"repository_id":700648272,"push_id":20412890192,"size":3,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/api-adoption","head":"d9d74729674c9b392d478c7d184409a03890bb87","before":"ba8ec41aab957fe6225ee3c4c4fa606400825962","commits":[{"sha":"af972e0a772069b4996cdd206d4e36defebd2453","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Fix markdown syntax in "Mark API as experimental"

Four spaces are needed in admonitions, not two.

Amends 8332e457e11fc3feb81ff9d8a1eaf6a500e57124.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/af972e0a772069b4996cdd206d4e36defebd2453"},{"sha":"da41b6a8ea4d4e355159e9c791111c445cc6805c","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Call load_customisations! automatically, lazily

I've been wary about doing lazy application of load_customisations!, but
with the @noinline addition the calls are really cheap: only 1.5ns
according to Chairmarks on my machine. Thus, the cost of calling it
after initialisation will be negligeable for any non-trivial function.

Having rolled this around further, other than printing I think only
withfaces calls actually require loading of user styles to be valid.

With these two realisations, calling load_customisations! lazily as
appropriate seems like a bit of a no-brainer.

We'll probably want to add this to public API eventually for
non-text/html output implementations, but we can wait to see how this is
bourne out first.","distinct":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/da41b6a8ea4d4e355159e9c791111c445cc6805c"},{"sha":"d9d74729674c9b392d478c7d184409a03890bb87","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Adopt Base's annotated types/functions as API

This opens the door to shuffling parts currently implemented in Base
into this stdlib without breaking any public APIs.

It would be nice to actually show the relevant Annotated* docstrings,
but unfortunately due to
https://github.com/JuliaDocs/Documenter.jl/issues/1781 it's a bit
difficult to actually do so. We should re-visit this later.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/d9d74729674c9b392d478c7d184409a03890bb87"}]}

```julia
help?> thing
# Observe working syntax highlighting

julia> using StyledStrings

help?> thing
# Syntax highlighting breaks

julia> using Markdown

help?> thing
# Syntax highlighting works again
```

Checking `Base.loaded_modules_order` it seems like two versions of `StyledStrings`, `JuliaSyntaxHighlighting`, and `Markdown` are loaded by doing this:

```julia
julia> Base.loaded_modules_order
22-element Vector{Module}:
 Core
 Base
 Main
 FileWatching
 Libdl
 Artifacts
 SHA
 Sockets
 LinearAlgebra
 OpenBLAS_jll
 libblastrampoline_jll
 Random
 Base64
 StyledStrings
 JuliaSyntaxHighlighting
 Markdown
 InteractiveUtils
 Unicode
 REPL
 StyledStrings
 JuliaSyntaxHighlighting
 Markdown
 
julia> length(unique(Base.loaded_modules_order))
22
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/comments/2378343715/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

release-1.11"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2024-06-24T17:37:19Z","updated_at":"2024-08-02T10:22:52Z","closed_at":"2024-07-24T20:39:50Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/54919","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54919","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54919.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54919.patch","merged_at":"2024-07-24T20:39:50Z"},"body":"As raised in <https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/57#issuecomment-2097457142>, when the eltype of an iterator is non-concrete, `_isannotated` will return false. To properly check such cases, we need to see if any of the elements of the iterator are annotated.

This is a bit of an interesting case, since:
- Most of the time it shouldn't be hit, we can reasonably expect most iterables to infer as producing concrete types
- The eltype of the iterator is (generally) known at compile-time, and so in any case other than the ambiguous non-concrete one, this check remains able to be done at compile-time.

With this change, join always preserves annotations. The compromise made is that iterators with non-concrete eltypes can result in join inferring a union return type (i.e. type instability with non-concrete iterators), but that doesn't seem too bad to me (I don't see how we could be completely type stable without concrete types here).

#### Before

![image](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/assets/20903656/a7b109ca-a9f1-4361-a9f7-618aa8ceba88)

#### After

![image](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/assets/20903656/f70b62a1-37a8-4df8-85b8-cea26ad81757)

I'd be keen to hear people's thoughts {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72","id":2444889215,"node_id":"I_kwDOKcMLUM6Rugx_","number":72,"title":"Printing an `AnnotatedString` is `O(length_string * num_annotations)`","user":{"login":"KristofferC","id":1282691,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyODI2OTE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1282691?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC","html_url":"https://github.com/KristofferC","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-08-02T12:45:20Z","updated_at":"2024-08-02T14:59:38Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"There are many cases where the function at https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/c6732a79494f604e0f320ea451856a1c10511659/base/strings/annotated.jl#L396-L401 gets called a number of times proportional to the length of the string (leading to quadratic behavior).

https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/blob/f7af623e050b537ab2188d38a3e28025ba1cbab7/src/regioniterator.jl#L52 is one such place.

When you iterate an `AnnotatedString` character by character (as e.g. `escape_string` does in Base) it also shows up.

I think it would be good to:

- do some pre processing of the annotations so that there is no need to do this type of random access lookup of them
- provide a better iterator over an `AnnotatedString` that uses something similar to `eachregion` instead of having to do everything from scratch for every character.

Printing a somewhat long string to stdout is noticeably quite slow.

![Aug-02-2024 14-41-23](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b92dd75f-a0d5-44f3-9626-fd49b893cb99)

Some code to generate a long string:

```julia
using Random

function generate_annot_string(n)
    colors = [:yellow, :green, :blue, :red]
    offset = 1
    annotations = Tuple{UnitRange{Int64}, Pair{Symbol, Any}}[]
    while true
        col = rand(1:length(colors))
        l = rand(1:10)
        offset + l > n && break
        push!(annotations, (offset:(offset+l), :face => colors[col]))
        offset += l
    end
    s = randstring(n)
    return Base.AnnotatedString(s, annotations)
end

str = generate_annot_string(100000)

It used to be a sorted list so `searchsortedfirst` could be used and came to the conclusion that this made the semantics worse and we should remove ordering assumptions, with sorting being something that could be done on-demand as a preprocessing step when needed.

Using the example you provided, `@time show(stdout, MIME("text/plain"), str)` gives a whopping 2.2s on my machine. The current (unoptimised) `eachregion`-backed `@time println(str)` gives a butter but still unimpressive time of 0.4s.

I've just had a go at optimising `eachregion` by pre-sorting the annotations. With my changes, `@time println(str)` is `O(n log n)` and takes 0.015s. Much better!

That doesn't help with `show` being char-by-char and still `O(n m)`, but I think it shows a path to acceptable performance.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/comments/2265594696/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

That's pretty underwhelming.

We can improve this to `O(n log n)` by pre-sorting the list of annotations, and working with it instead. A bit more complexity is needed to do this while preserving the semantics, but it can be worth it for long strings. With a 100,000 char string with 20,000 annotations, print time goes from ~0.4s to 0.015s on my machine.

                     "; ",","path":"stdlib/Profile/src/Profile.jl","commit_id":"6eef6d0a47ad0f24e6d1d01a4b332a0a5eb9bd5c","original_commit_id":"6eef6d0a47ad0f24e6d1d01a4b332a0a5eb9bd5c","user":{"login":"tecosaur","id":20903656,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwOTAzNjU2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20903656?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur","html_url":"https://github.com/tecosaur","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Ok, GitHub doesn't seem to let me comment on lines more than three lines away from the diff changes, but this is actually pursuant to line 945 (962 with your patch):

```julia
strs[i] = ltruncto(strs[i], cols)
```

There are two problems here, one existing, one introduced.

Firstly, the existing problem: `ltruncto` / `rtruncto` mix up indices and `textwidth` and that means that `textwidth(ltruncto(str, n)) <= n` doesn't always hold. For example `textwidth(ltruncto("🍕" ^ 15))` is `19`.

Now the new problem: by introducing `sprint` + `printstyled` above, `strs[i]` now contains ANSI escape codes that are counted by `length`/`textwidth` but don't actually increase the width of the string. This is going to cause lines to be systematically over-shortened.

```julia-repl
julia> textwidth(sprint(io -> printstyled(io, "hey"), context=stdout))
6

{"repository_id":700648272,"push_id":19611914077,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/presort-in-eachregion","head":"1044edfb82086e12d466923b596c74cca2757701","before":"b74d3968aad834d74952e098f57dd2d7d24b7eb1","commits":[{"sha":"1044edfb82086e12d466923b596c74cca2757701","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Refactor eachregion to be O(n log n) not O(n^2)

Since we removed the ordering restriction on annotations to improve the
semantics of annotation modification, each `annotations(str)` call
became `O(n)` which is fine for a once off, but use it in a loop as
`eachregion` does and now it's `O(n m)`. That's pretty underwhelming.

We can improve this to `O(n log n)` by pre-sorting the list of
annotations, and working with it instead. A bit more complexity is
needed to do this while preserving the semantics, but it can be worth it
for long strings. With a 100,000 char string with 20,000 annotations,
print time goes from ~0.4s to ~0.01s on my machine.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/commits/1044edfb82086e12d466923b596c74cca2757701"}]}

That's pretty underwhelming.

We can improve this to `O(n log n)` by pre-sorting the list of annotations, and working with it instead. A bit more complexity is needed to do this while preserving the semantics, but it can be worth it for long strings. With a 100,000 char string with 20,000 annotations, print time goes from ~0.4s to 0.015s on my machine.

we removed the ordering restriction on annotations to improve the semantics of annotation modification, each `annotations(str)` call became `O(n)` which is fine for a once off, but use it in a loop as `eachregion` does and now it's `O(n m)`. That's pretty underwhelming.

We can improve this to `O(n log n)` by pre-sorting the list of annotations, and working with it instead. A bit more complexity is needed to do this while preserving the semantics, but it can be worth it for long strings. With a 100,000 char string with 20,000 annotations, print time goes from ~0.4s to 0.015s on my machine.

-----

This improvement has been prompted by {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72","id":2444889215,"node_id":"I_kwDOKcMLUM6Rugx_","number":72,"title":"Showing an `AnnotatedString` is `O(length_string * num_annotations)`","user":{"login":"KristofferC","id":1282691,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyODI2OTE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1282691?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC","html_url":"https://github.com/KristofferC","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-08-02T12:45:20Z","updated_at":"2024-08-04T09:05:34Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"There are many cases where the function at https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/c6732a79494f604e0f320ea451856a1c10511659/base/strings/annotated.jl#L396-L401 gets called a number of times proportional to the length of the string (leading to quadratic behavior).

https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/blob/f7af623e050b537ab2188d38a3e28025ba1cbab7/src/regioniterator.jl#L52 is one such place.

When you iterate an `AnnotatedString` character by character (as e.g. `escape_string` does in Base) it also shows up.

I think it would be good to:

- do some pre processing of the annotations so that there is no need to do this type of random access lookup of them
- provide a better iterator over an `AnnotatedString` that uses something similar to `eachregion` instead of having to do everything from scratch for every character.

Printing a somewhat long string to stdout is noticeably quite slow.

![Aug-02-2024 14-41-23](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b92dd75f-a0d5-44f3-9626-fd49b893cb99)

Some code to generate a long string:

```julia
using Random

function generate_annot_string(n)
    colors = [:yellow, :green, :blue, :red]
    offset = 1
    annotations = Tuple{UnitRange{Int64}, Pair{Symbol, Any}}[]
    while true
        col = rand(1:length(colors))
        l = rand(1:10)
        offset + l > n && break
        push!(annotations, (offset:(offset+l), :face => colors[col]))
        offset += l
    end
    s = randstring(n)
    return Base.AnnotatedString(s, annotations)
end

str = generate_annot_string(100000)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/comments/2267447850","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72#issuecomment-2267447850","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72","id":2267447850,"node_id":"IC_kwDOKcMLUM6HJoIq","user":{"login":"tecosaur","id":20903656,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwOTAzNjU2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20903656?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur","html_url":"https://github.com/tecosaur","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-04T09:05:33Z","updated_at":"2024-08-04T09:05:33Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"I wonder why the string limiting doesn't apply?

{"repository_id":700648272,"push_id":19616279054,"size":3,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/kc/import_uing","head":"58507e52d0d8e4665bc6a803f67149c126f56975","before":"faabf4e11fff8e150d3050d408ee4f77bc45df3b","commits":[{"sha":"c417262c652e73a69a5516ffdfdd812e36102250","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Refactor eachregion to be O(n log n) not O(n^2)

Since we removed the ordering restriction on annotations to improve the
semantics of annotation modification, each `annotations(str)` call
became `O(n)` which is fine for a once off, but use it in a loop as
`eachregion` does and now it's `O(n m)`. That's pretty underwhelming.

We can improve this to `O(n log n)` by pre-sorting the list of
annotations, and working with it instead. A bit more complexity is
needed to do this while preserving the semantics, but it can be worth it

an `AnnotatedString` is `O(length_string * num_annotations)`","user":{"login":"KristofferC","id":1282691,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyODI2OTE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1282691?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC","html_url":"https://github.com/KristofferC","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-08-02T12:45:20Z","updated_at":"2024-08-04T09:38:42Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"There are many cases where the function at https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/c6732a79494f604e0f320ea451856a1c10511659/base/strings/annotated.jl#L396-L401 gets called a number of times proportional to the length of the string (leading to quadratic behavior).

https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/blob/f7af623e050b537ab2188d38a3e28025ba1cbab7/src/regioniterator.jl#L52 is one such place.

When you iterate an `AnnotatedString` character by character (as e.g. `escape_string` does in Base) it also shows up.

I think it would be good to:

- do some pre processing of the annotations so that there is no need to do this type of random access lookup of them
- provide a better iterator over an `AnnotatedString` that uses something similar to `eachregion` instead of having to do everything from scratch for every character.

Printing a somewhat long string to stdout is noticeably quite slow.

![Aug-02-2024 14-41-23](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b92dd75f-a0d5-44f3-9626-fd49b893cb99)

Some code to generate a long string:

```julia
using Random

function generate_annot_string(n)
    colors = [:yellow, :green, :blue, :red]
    offset = 1
    annotations = Tuple{UnitRange{Int64}, Pair{Symbol, Any}}[]
    while true
        col = rand(1:length(colors))
        l = rand(1:10)
        offset + l > n && break
        push!(annotations, (offset:(offset+l), :face => colors[col]))
        offset += l
    end
    s = randstring(n)
    return Base.AnnotatedString(s, annotations)
end

str = generate_annot_string(100000)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/comments/2267464888","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72#issuecomment-2267464888","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72","id":2267464888,"node_id":"IC_kwDOKcMLUM6HJsS4","user":{"login":"tecosaur","id":20903656,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwOTAzNjU2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20903656?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur","html_url":"https://github.com/tecosaur","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-04T09:38:41Z","updated_at":"2024-08-04T09:38:41Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"Ok, that deserves it's own investigation, but I've just discovered why `show` is so much slower than `print`: While an optimized `escape_string` is defined, `Base.escape_string` isn't imported :facepalm: (I suppose this is another argument for #69).

With an added `Base.`, I now see:
```julia-repl
juila> @time show(stdout, MIME("text/plain"), str)
"... lots of output..."
 0.037248 seconds (552.72 k allocations: 26.695 MiB, 35.34% gc time)
```

So, as of #74 original issue raised here will be fixed.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/comments/2267464888/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"repository_id":700648272,"push_id":19616358316,"size":1,"distinct_size":0,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"6d3f44d01b8e31205252780b3f045271c879fc67","before":"58507e52d0d8e4665bc6a803f67149c126f56975","commits":[{"sha":"6d3f44d01b8e31205252780b3f045271c879fc67","author":{"email":"46f1a0bd5592a2f9244ca321b129902a06b53e03@tecosaur.net","name":"TEC"},"message":"Actually overload Base's escape_string

While part of the code brought over from Base (or rather a PR to Base)
in 745385d0ac85, it was missed when adding the relevant imports to
overload the Base methods.

The main impact of this was slow 3-arg show performance, as raised in

an `AnnotatedString` is `O(length_string * num_annotations)`","user":{"login":"KristofferC","id":1282691,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyODI2OTE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1282691?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC","html_url":"https://github.com/KristofferC","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KristofferC/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-08-02T12:45:20Z","updated_at":"2024-08-04T09:46:09Z","closed_at":"2024-08-04T09:46:09Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"There are many cases where the function at https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/c6732a79494f604e0f320ea451856a1c10511659/base/strings/annotated.jl#L396-L401 gets called a number of times proportional to the length of the string (leading to quadratic behavior).

https://github.com/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/blob/f7af623e050b537ab2188d38a3e28025ba1cbab7/src/regioniterator.jl#L52 is one such place.

When you iterate an `AnnotatedString` character by character (as e.g. `escape_string` does in Base) it also shows up.

I think it would be good to:

- do some pre processing of the annotations so that there is no need to do this type of random access lookup of them
- provide a better iterator over an `AnnotatedString` that uses something similar to `eachregion` instead of having to do everything from scratch for every character.

Printing a somewhat long string to stdout is noticeably quite slow.

![Aug-02-2024 14-41-23](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b92dd75f-a0d5-44f3-9626-fd49b893cb99)

Some code to generate a long string:

```julia
using Random

function generate_annot_string(n)
    colors = [:yellow, :green, :blue, :red]
    offset = 1
    annotations = Tuple{UnitRange{Int64}, Pair{Symbol, Any}}[]
    while true
        col = rand(1:length(colors))
        l = rand(1:10)
        offset + l > n && break
        push!(annotations, (offset:(offset+l), :face => colors[col]))
        offset += l
    end
    s = randstring(n)
    return Base.AnnotatedString(s, annotations)
end

str = generate_annot_string(100000)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/72/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}
